cards.text:
The median for grouped data is formed by using the formula:
Median = {tex}l+\left(\frac{\frac{n}{2}-\mathrm{cf}}{f}\right) \times h{/tex},
where symbols have their usual meanings.

The mean for grouped data can be found by:
the step deviation method: {tex}\bar{x}=a+\left(\frac{\Sigma f_{i} u_{i}}{\Sigma f_{i}}\right) \times h{/tex}   where {tex}u_i= { x_i-a \over h}{/tex}  , a= assumed mean and h= class width

The mean for grouped data can be found by:
the assumed mean method: {tex}\bar{x}=a+\frac{\sum f_{i} d_{i}}{\Sigma f_{i}} {/tex}  where {tex}d_i=x_i-a{/tex}, a = Assumed mean

The mean for grouped data can be found by:
the direct method: {tex}\bar{x}=\frac{\sum f_{i} x_{i}}{\Sigma f_{i}}{/tex}

The mode for grouped data can be found by using the formula:
Mode = {tex}l+\left(\frac{f_{1}-f_{0}}{2 f_{1}-f_{0}-f_{2}}\right) \times h{/tex}
where symbols have their usual meanings.
